svg.text: The history file that betrays everything
One read-only .bash_history file is enough to compromise the entire infrastructure

g.text: Lara, a developer, clicks a phishing link. Her account is compromised.
The attacker lists the command history. They find plaintext credentials:
The private SSH key is sitting on the workstation. The attacker connects directly
to the production server — no brute-force, no scan, no alert triggered.
The MySQL password recovered from the history works. The attacker
exfiltrates 2.3 million customer records in under 20 minutes.
A backdoor is planted, log traces are wiped… then they disappear.

svg.text: No network scan. No CVE exploit. No SIEM alert triggered.
The attacker used only legitimate credentials found in plaintext.
